meaning: v. 放弃，抛弃；n. 放纵
example: He abandoned his wife and children.
The professor gave an abstract lecture on philosophy.

meaning: v. 容纳；提供住宿；适应
example: The hotel can accommodate up to 500 guests.
She acquired a new skill through practice.

meaning: adj. 充分的，足够的；适当的
example: The company has adequate resources for the project.

meaning: v. 提倡，主张；n. 倡导者

example: The government allocated funds for education.

meaning: adj. 模棱两可的，含糊的
example: The instructions were ambiguous and confusing.
We anticipate a significant increase in sales.
It was apparent that he was lying.

meaning: v. 接近；n. 方法，途径
example: We need a new approach to solve this problem.
Please wear appropriate clothing for the event.
Problems may arise during the implementation.
I assume you have read the instructions.
